paper_title: Highly selective CO<sub>2</sub> photoreduction to CO over g-C<sub>3</sub>N<sub>4</sub>/Bi<sub>2</sub>WO<sub>6</sub> composites under visible light

catalyst.source: Highly selective CO<sub>2</sub> photoreduction to CO over g-C<sub>3</sub>N<sub>4</sub>/Bi<sub>2</sub>WO<sub>6</sub> composites under visible light
catalyst.context: Highly selective CO<sub>2</sub> photoreduction to CO over g-C<sub>3</sub>N<sub>4</sub>/Bi<sub>2</sub>WO<sub>6</sub> composites under visible light

light source.source: The g-C 3 N 4 content and synthesis parameters of these composites have been tuned to obtain the optimized photocatalytic activity with a peak CO production rate of 5.19 mmol g A1 h A1 under visible light irradiation at room temperature, which was 22 and 6.4 times that on pure g-C 3 N 4 and Bi 2 WO 6 , respectively.
light source.context: CO 2 is highly stable and therefore extremely difficult to be reduced at room temperature even by photocatalysis.Herein, a series of g-C 3 N 4 /Bi 2 WO 6 composites have been synthesized through a facile in situ hydrothermal approach, which demonstrated greatly enhanced response to visible light, and consequently a remarkably enhanced CO 2 selective photoreduction to CO.The g-C 3 N 4 content and synthesis parameters of these composites have been tuned to obtain the optimized photocatalytic activity with a peak CO production rate of 5.19 mmol g A1 h A1 under visible light irradiation at room temperature, which was 22 and 6.4 times that on pure g-C 3 N 4 and Bi 2 WO 6 , respectively.Based on the matched band energy potentials between g-C 3 N 4 and Bi 2 WO 6 in the synthesized composites, a possible Z-scheme mechanism, which features a significantly promoted separation of photo-generated carriers under visible light irradiation by the composites, has been proposed to account for the distinctive CO 2 photoreduction performance.

lamp.source: Conditions: 0.1 g catalyst; reaction temperature, 25 C; ambient pressure; 300 W Xe lamp (l 420 nm).
lamp.context: A novel kind of g-C 3 N 4 /Bi 2 WO 6 has been fabricated using a conventional hydrothermal method.These composites exhibit signicant visible light sensitivity and highly efficient separation and transfer of charge carriers, i.e., photogenerated electron-hole pairs, across the interface between g-C 3 N 4 and Bi 2 WO 6 .The as-prepared catalysts show greatly enhanced photocatalytic performance in the photoreduction of CO 2 with gaseous water as reducing agent and up to 100 CO selectivity under visible light with no CH 4 or other C1 intermediate products being detected.The optimum photocatalytic CO evolution rate on g-C 3 N 4 -0.1/Bi 2 WO 6 reaches 5.19 mmol g A1 h A1 , around 22 and 6.4 times that on the separate g-C 3 N 4 and Bi 2 WO 6 , respectively.The catalytic stability is not as satisfactory as expected and further investigation on this is in progress.This work shows a promising prospect for the utilization of g-C 3 N 4 / Bi 2 WO 6 as a visible-light-driven photocatalyst for CO 2 photoconversion without the addition of any noble metal co-catalyst. Fig.1XRD patterns of g-C 3 N 4 , Bi 2 WO 6 , and g-C 3 N 4 /Bi 2 WO 6 samples. Fig. 2 N 2 adsorption-desorption isotherms of g-C 3 N 4 , Bi 2 WO 6 and g-C 3 N 4 -x/Bi 2 WO 6 samples. Fig. 3 SEM images of (a) g-C 3 N 4 and (b) g-C 3 N 4 -0.1/Bi 2 WO 6 composite; (c) TEM image and SAED pattern (inset) and (d) HRTEM image of g-C 3 N 4 -0.1/Bi 2 WO 6 sample. Fig. 4 (a) UV-visible diffuse reflectance spectra of the as-prepared samples.(b) Photoluminescence spectra at an excitation wavelength of 370 nm. Fig. 5 Mott-Schottky plots collected for g-C 3 N 4 (a) and Bi 2 WO 6 (b) at a frequency of 962 Hz in the dark. Fig. 6 Photo-responses of g-C 3 N 4 , g-C 3 N 4 -0.1/Bi 2 WO 6 and pure Bi 2 WO 6 electrodes.All the electrodes were evaluated in 0.1 M Na 2 SO 4 aqueous solution under visible light irradiation using Ag/AgCl as reference electrode. Fig. 7 X-ray photoelectron spectra of (a) Bi 2 WO 6 and g-C 3 N 4 -0.1/Bi 2 WO 6 , and high-resolution spectra of (b) C 1s, (c) O 1s, (d) W 4f, and (e) Bi 4f. Fig. 8 (a) Time-dependent CO generation over g-C 3 N 4 -0.1/Bi 2 WO 6 composites prepared at various hydrothermal temperatures.(b) CO yield over g-C 3 N 4 -0.1/Bi 2 WO 6 composites prepared at 160 C for various durations.(c) Photocatalytic conversion of CO 2 over g-C 3 N 4x/Bi 2 WO 6 composites with different amounts of g-C 3 N 4 .(d) Stability study of photocatalytic CO evolution over the g-C 3 N 4 -0.1/Bi 2 WO 6 sample under visible light irradiation.Conditions: 0.1 g catalyst; reaction temperature, 25 C; ambient pressure; 300 W Xe lamp (l 420 nm). 42,43C 3 N 4 -ZnWO 4 , 44 C 3 N 4 -Bi 2 WO 6 , 45 C 3 N 4 -graphene 46 etc. Bi 2 WO 6 is one of the simplest members of the Aurivillius family, composed of accumulated layers of cornersharing WO 6 octahedral sheets and bismuth oxide sheets.With a layered structure, Bi 2 WO 6 exhibits excellent photocatalytic activity under visible light and has been widely used in splitting water to produce H 2 and O 2 This journal is The Royal Society of Chemistry 2015 This work was nancially supported by the National Key Basic Research Program of China (2013CB933200), National 863 plans projects (2012AA062703), National Natural Science Foundation of China (21177137) and the Key Program for Science and Technology Commission of Shanghai Municipality (11JC1413400).
